rows.title: ClinicSentry
rows.subtitle: compliance middleware for clinical AI agents
rows.desc: An open-source Python library that sits between any agent framework — LangGraph, CrewAI, OpenAI Agents SDK, Claude SDK, MCP — and the outside world, enforcing clinical guardrails without touching agent code. A **PHI firewall** with adversarial normalization (homoglyphs, zero-width characters, base64 payloads), a **risk-tiered escalation router** that fails closed, and a **tamper-evident HMAC audit chain**, mapped clause-by-clause to HIPAA, FDA TPLC, IEC 62304 and the EU AI Act. Backed by a separate benchmark harness: six benchmark families covering detection F1, adversarial robustness, latency and compliance correctness.
rows.proof: ~0.7 ms p95 PHI scan on 3 KB notes · zero LLM calls on the hot path · 4 regulatory frameworks auto-checked

rows.title: StressBench
rows.subtitle: when agents pass the parser but fail the task
rows.desc: An evaluation harness that stress-tests LLM agents under injected faults and reports two numbers side by side: **strict_pass** (the answer matched the format and tool-call protocol) and **verified** (an action-metamorphic verifier recomputed the true end-state from the tool outputs the agent actually saw). The gap between them — **format-without-correctness** — is the headline metric. Compares ReAct, native function-calling, calibration and verifier-in-the-loop remediation agents, with bootstrap CIs, McNemar + Holm correction, and calibration error.
rows.proof: 16 verifier task families · 9 injected fault types · dual strict/verified reporting

rows.title: Icarus
rows.subtitle: a JARVIS-style assistant that runs entirely on my Mac
rows.desc: Local-first, multi-agent personal assistant with a full voice loop: mlx-whisper speech-to-text on the Apple Silicon GPU, an **orchestrator routing across specialist agents** — email, calendar, news, finance, code, Mac control, memory — on Ollama qwen3, and Kokoro TTS answering in a British JARVIS voice. **Five risk tiers gate every action**: risky operations pause on an approval queue, destructive ones are hard-blocked. Reachable from my iPhone as a PWA over LAN; escalates heavy tasks to Claude only when told to.
rows.proof: ~0.3 s transcription · ~2 s speech synthesis warm · $0/month runtime · 5 risk tiers with approval queue

rows.title: Krystina
rows.subtitle: a Discord ops assistant with a human veto on every message
rows.desc: The operations bot for a real motorcycle club. It plans the monthly group-ride messaging pipeline from Google Calendar, computes deterministic ride-role assignments, and handles plain-English admin requests — *"move the poll reminder to July 2"*, *"DM Wes to bring the first-aid kit"* — routed through gpt-4o-mini. **Nothing reaches the club without an Accept / Change / Cancel confirmation** DM'd to both admins: buttons persist for 72 hours across redeploys, plain-English edits are applied and re-confirmed, and stalled pipeline steps self-heal on later runs.
rows.proof: running since Feb 2026 · every outbound message human-approved · state survives restarts & redeploys

rows.title: Icarus Job Search
rows.subtitle: an operator console that can't lie on your resume
rows.desc: A local-first Mac desktop app (Tauri + Next.js + FastAPI + SQLite) for running a serious job search. Pulls real openings from public ATS APIs (Greenhouse, Ashby), scores fit and visa sponsorship with negation-aware detection, and tailors resumes under a hard **truthfulness constraint: every exported bullet must trace back to the master resume or an approved fact, or the export is blocked**. Gmail OAuth tokens live in the macOS Keychain, outreach drafts require human approval, and LinkedIn automation deliberately doesn't exist.
rows.proof: every resume claim provenance-checked · approval gates on all sends · secrets in Keychain, never on disk

rows.title: Agent Skills
rows.subtitle: packaged judgment for AI coding agents
rows.desc: A library of 15 reusable skills for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf): TDD enforcement, structured root-cause analysis, CI/CD generation, resilience patterns, container optimization, secrets management. Each skill encodes a senior engineer's checklist as instructions an agent actually follows — installable globally or per-workspace with one command.
rows.proof: 15 skills across the SDLC · single-command install · works across agent tools
